channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17810] lfs migrate -m leaves 1-stripe dir in a two-level "striped" form
item.description:
lfs migrate -m leaves 1-stripe dir in a two-level "striped" form:

[root@rocky tests]# mkdir /mnt/lustre/migrate-test
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/migrate-test/
lmv_stripe_count: 0 lmv_stripe_offset: 0 lmv_hash_type: none
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs migrate -m 1 /mnt/lustre/migrate-test/
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/migrate-test/
lmv_stripe_count: 1 lmv_stripe_offset: 1 lmv_hash_type: crush,fixed
mdtidx		 FID[seq:oid:ver]
     1		 [0x240000400:0x2:0x0]		
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs path2fid /mnt/lustre/migrate-test/
[0x240000402:0x1:0x0]
[root@rocky tests]# debugfs -R "ls -l /REMOTE_PARENT_DIR/0x240000402:0x1:0x0" /dev/mapper/mds2_flakey 
debugfs 1.46.2.wc5 (26-Mar-2022)
  25047   40755 (2)      0      0    4096  3-May-2024 15:41 .
  25001   40755 (18)      0      0    4096  3-May-2024 15:42 ..
  25048   40755 (18)      0      0    4096  3-May-2024 15:41 [0x240000400:0x2:0x0]:0

[root@rocky tests]#

the migration process does not end by a conversion the result dir into "plain" ldiskfs dir.
The dir is accessible, it is possible to create and delete files in the dir and remove the dir itself w/o problems.
item.summary: lfs migrate -m leaves 1-stripe dir in a two-level "striped" form

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Etienne AUJAMES <eaujames@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57812
Subject: LU-17810 dne: dir restripe without fixed hash flag
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 10874d5ea17692b250c4655a888eb8efbb83375c

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57117/
Subject: LU-17810 dne: dir restripe without fixed hash flag
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 392f558f405238d1b455256650a6aaa26fa7ab6e

here is a demonstration of the fix:

[root@rocky tests]# mkdir /mnt/lustre/testdir
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs migrate -m 1 --clear-fixed /mnt/lustre/testdir
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/testdir
lmv_stripe_count: 0 lmv_stripe_offset: 1 lmv_hash_type: none
mdtidx		 FID[seq:oid:ver]
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs migrate -m 0 /mnt/lustre/testdir
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/testdir
lmv_stripe_count: 1 lmv_stripe_offset: 0 lmv_hash_type: crush,fixed
mdtidx		 FID[seq:oid:ver]
     0		 [0x200000400:0x2:0x0]		
[root@rocky tests]#

"Alexander Zarochentsev <alexander.zarochentsev@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57117
Subject: LU-17810 dne: dir restripe without fixed hash flag
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a873abc7bce8d5623a01a00926de828f97fbc661

> Any directory restriped to one-dir will get "fixed", which makes it not plain.
A plain dir from one MDT gets converted into a 1-striped dir on another MDT w/o a simple way to make it "plain" again. The two-level dir structure would obviously have some negative performance effect (I have no test results though) w/o the benefits of DNE.

> well, it is still not clear why there is no workaround for just a simple case of moving normal dir to another MDT.
Zam, from what I read in the LU-14459 commit message is that it is a 'feature', not a bug. Any directory restripesd to one-dir will get "fixed", which makes it not plain. I guess though, what there should be is some option to "migrate" a "fixed" dir anyway.

commit 4c2514f4832801374092f3a48c755248af345566
Author: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@whamcloud.com>
Date:  Thu Apr 29 05:30:00 2021 +0800

  LU-14459 mdt: support fixed directory layout
   
  User may not want directories split automatically in some cases:
  *.directory migrated.
  * directory restriped.
   
  To support this, an LMV flag LMV_HASH_FLAG_FIXED is added, and it will
  be set on migrated/restriped directories. NB, if directory is migrated
  or restriped to a one-stripe directory, it won't be transformed into a
  plain directory, because this flag needs to be kept.
   
  Update sanity 230q.

well, it is still not clear why there is no workaround for just a simple case of moving normal dir to another MDT.

it appears "fixed" flag doesn't allow the 1-striped dir to be converted into "normal" dir:

int mdd_dir_layout_shrink(const struct lu_env *env,
...
        if (le32_to_cpu(lmu->lum_stripe_count) == 1 && !lmv_is_fixed(lmv)) {
                rc = mdd_1sd_collapse(env, pobj, obj, stripe, attr, &xattrs,
                                      mlc, &lname, handle);
                if (rc)
                        GOTO(stop_trans, rc);
        }

removal of the check :

diff --git a/lustre/mdd/mdd_dir.c b/lustre/mdd/mdd_dir.c
index 43532bd51e..adeee897ca 100644
--- a/lustre/mdd/mdd_dir.c
+++ b/lustre/mdd/mdd_dir.c
@@ -4971,7 +4971,7 @@ int mdd_dir_layout_shrink(const struct lu_env *env,
        if (rc)
                GOTO(stop_trans, rc);
 
-       if (le32_to_cpu(lmu->lum_stripe_count) == 1 && !lmv_is_fixed(lmv)) {
+       if (le32_to_cpu(lmu->lum_stripe_count) == 1 /* && !lmv_is_fixed(lmv) */) {
                rc = mdd_1sd_collapse(env, pobj, obj, stripe, attr, &xattrs,
                                      mlc, &lname, handle);
                if (rc)

allows the migration complete conversion :

[root@rocky tests]# mkdir /mnt/lustre/migrate-test
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/migrate-test/
lmv_stripe_count: 0 lmv_stripe_offset: 0 lmv_hash_type: none
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs migrate -m 1 /mnt/lustre/migrate-test/
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/migrate-test/
lmv_stripe_count: 0 lmv_stripe_offset: 1 lmv_hash_type: none
[root@rocky tests]#

Probably the flag should not be set during the re-striping.
